tools.name: actionAddListenerCertificates.getAddListenerCertificates
tools.description:
Adds the specified SSL server certificate to the certificate list for the specified HTTPS or TLS listener.

If the certificate in already in the certificate list, the call is successful but the certificate is not added again.

For more information, see HTTPS listeners in the Application Load Balancers Guide or TLS listeners in the Network Load Balancers Guide.

tools.name: actionAddListenerCertificates.postAddListenerCertificates
tools.description:
Adds the specified SSL server certificate to the certificate list for the specified HTTPS or TLS listener.

If the certificate in already in the certificate list, the call is successful but the certificate is not added again.

For more information, see HTTPS listeners in the Application Load Balancers Guide or TLS listeners in the Network Load Balancers Guide.

tools.name: actionAddTags.getAddTags
tools.description:
Adds the specified tags to the specified Elastic Load Balancing resource. You can tag your Application Load Balancers, Network Load Balancers, Gateway Load Balancers, target groups, listeners, and rules.

Each tag consists of a key and an optional value. If a resource already has a tag with the same key, AddTags updates its value.

tools.name: actionAddTags.postAddTags
tools.description:
Adds the specified tags to the specified Elastic Load Balancing resource. You can tag your Application Load Balancers, Network Load Balancers, Gateway Load Balancers, target groups, listeners, and rules.

Each tag consists of a key and an optional value. If a resource already has a tag with the same key, AddTags updates its value.

tools.name: actionCreateListener.getCreateListener
tools.description:
Creates a listener for the specified Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer.

For more information, see the following:

  • Listeners for your Application Load Balancers

  • Listeners for your Network Load Balancers

  • Listeners for your Gateway Load Balancers

This operation is idempotent, which means that it completes at most one time. If you attempt to create multiple listeners with the same settings, each call succeeds.

tools.name: actionCreateListener.postCreateListener
tools.description:
Creates a listener for the specified Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer.

For more information, see the following:

  • Listeners for your Application Load Balancers

  • Listeners for your Network Load Balancers

  • Listeners for your Gateway Load Balancers

This operation is idempotent, which means that it completes at most one time. If you attempt to create multiple listeners with the same settings, each call succeeds.

tools.name: actionCreateLoadBalancer.getCreateLoadBalancer
tools.description:
Creates an Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer.

For more information, see the following:

  • Application Load Balancers

  • Network Load Balancers

  • Gateway Load Balancers

This operation is idempotent, which means that it completes at most one time. If you attempt to create multiple load balancers with the same settings, each call succeeds.

tools.name: actionCreateLoadBalancer.postCreateLoadBalancer
tools.description:
Creates an Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer.

For more information, see the following:

  • Application Load Balancers

  • Network Load Balancers

  • Gateway Load Balancers

This operation is idempotent, which means that it completes at most one time. If you attempt to create multiple load balancers with the same settings, each call succeeds.

tools.name: actionCreateRule.getCreateRule
tools.description:
Creates a rule for the specified listener. The listener must be associated with an Application Load Balancer.

Each rule consists of a priority, one or more actions, and one or more conditions. Rules are evaluated in priority order, from the lowest value to the highest value. When the conditions for a rule are met, its actions are performed. If the conditions for no rules are met, the actions for the default rule are performed. For more information, see Listener rules in the Application Load Balancers Guide.

tools.name: actionCreateRule.postCreateRule
tools.description:
Creates a rule for the specified listener. The listener must be associated with an Application Load Balancer.

Each rule consists of a priority, one or more actions, and one or more conditions. Rules are evaluated in priority order, from the lowest value to the highest value. When the conditions for a rule are met, its actions are performed. If the conditions for no rules are met, the actions for the default rule are performed. For more information, see Listener rules in the Application Load Balancers Guide.

tools.name: actionCreateTargetGroup.getCreateTargetGroup
tools.description:
Creates a target group.

For more information, see the following:

  • Target groups for your Application Load Balancers

  • Target groups for your Network Load Balancers

  • Target groups for your Gateway Load Balancers

This operation is idempotent, which means that it completes at most one time. If you attempt to create multiple target groups with the same settings, each call succeeds.

tools.name: actionCreateTargetGroup.postCreateTargetGroup
tools.description:
Creates a target group.

For more information, see the following:

  • Target groups for your Application Load Balancers

  • Target groups for your Network Load Balancers

  • Target groups for your Gateway Load Balancers

This operation is idempotent, which means that it completes at most one time. If you attempt to create multiple target groups with the same settings, each call succeeds.

tools.name: actionDeleteListener.getDeleteListener
tools.description:
Deletes the specified listener.

Alternatively, your listener is deleted when you delete the load balancer to which it is attached.

tools.name: actionDeleteListener.postDeleteListener
tools.description:
Deletes the specified listener.

Alternatively, your listener is deleted when you delete the load balancer to which it is attached.

tools.name: actionDeleteLoadBalancer.getDeleteLoadBalancer
tools.description:
Deletes the specified Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer. Deleting a load balancer also deletes its listeners.

You can't delete a load balancer if deletion protection is enabled. If the load balancer does not exist or has already been deleted, the call succeeds.

Deleting a load balancer does not affect its registered targets. For example, your EC2 instances continue to run and are still registered to their target groups. If you no longer need these EC2 instances, you can stop or terminate them.

tools.name: actionDeleteLoadBalancer.postDeleteLoadBalancer
tools.description:
Deletes the specified Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer. Deleting a load balancer also deletes its listeners.

You can't delete a load balancer if deletion protection is enabled. If the load balancer does not exist or has already been deleted, the call succeeds.

Deleting a load balancer does not affect its registered targets. For example, your EC2 instances continue to run and are still registered to their target groups. If you no longer need these EC2 instances, you can stop or terminate them.

tools.name: actionDeleteRule.getDeleteRule
tools.description:
Deletes the specified rule.

You can't delete the default rule.

tools.name: actionDeleteRule.postDeleteRule
tools.description:
Deletes the specified rule.

You can't delete the default rule.

tools.name: actionDeleteTargetGroup.getDeleteTargetGroup
tools.description:
Deletes the specified target group.

You can delete a target group if it is not referenced by any actions. Deleting a target group also deletes any associated health checks. Deleting a target group does not affect its registered targets. For example, any EC2 instances continue to run until you stop or terminate them.

tools.name: actionDeleteTargetGroup.postDeleteTargetGroup
tools.description:
Deletes the specified target group.

You can delete a target group if it is not referenced by any actions. Deleting a target group also deletes any associated health checks. Deleting a target group does not affect its registered targets. For example, any EC2 instances continue to run until you stop or terminate them.

tools.name: actionDeregisterTargets.getDeregisterTargets
tools.description: Deregisters the specified targets from the specified target group. After the targets are deregistered, they no longer receive traffic from the load balancer.

tools.name: actionDeregisterTargets.postDeregisterTargets
tools.description: Deregisters the specified targets from the specified target group. After the targets are deregistered, they no longer receive traffic from the load balancer.

tools.name: actionDescribeAccountLimits.getDescribeAccountLimits
tools.description:
Describes the current Elastic Load Balancing resource limits for your Amazon Web Services account.

For more information, see the following:

  • Quotas for your Application Load Balancers

  • Quotas for your Network Load Balancers

  • Quotas for your Gateway Load Balancers

tools.name: actionDescribeAccountLimits.postDescribeAccountLimits
tools.description:
Describes the current Elastic Load Balancing resource limits for your Amazon Web Services account.

For more information, see the following:

  • Quotas for your Application Load Balancers

  • Quotas for your Network Load Balancers

  • Quotas for your Gateway Load Balancers

tools.name: actionDescribeListenerCertificates.getDescribeListenerCertificates
tools.description:
Describes the default certificate and the certificate list for the specified HTTPS or TLS listener.

If the default certificate is also in the certificate list, it appears twice in the results (once with IsDefault set to true and once with IsDefault set to false).

For more information, see SSL certificates in the Application Load Balancers Guide or Server certificates in the Network Load Balancers Guide.

tools.name: actionDescribeListenerCertificates.postDescribeListenerCertificates
tools.description:
Describes the default certificate and the certificate list for the specified HTTPS or TLS listener.

If the default certificate is also in the certificate list, it appears twice in the results (once with IsDefault set to true and once with IsDefault set to false).

For more information, see SSL certificates in the Application Load Balancers Guide or Server certificates in the Network Load Balancers Guide.

tools.name: actionDescribeListeners.getDescribeListeners
tools.description: Describes the specified listeners or the listeners for the specified Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer. You must specify either a load balancer or one or more listeners.

tools.name: actionDescribeListeners.postDescribeListeners
tools.description: Describes the specified listeners or the listeners for the specified Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer. You must specify either a load balancer or one or more listeners.

tools.name: actionDescribeLoadBalancerAttributes.getDescribeLoadBalancerAttributes
tools.description:
Describes the attributes for the specified Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer.

For more information, see the following:

  • Load balancer attributes in the Application Load Balancers Guide

  • Load balancer attributes in the Network Load Balancers Guide

  • Load balancer attributes in the Gateway Load Balancers Guide

tools.name: actionDescribeLoadBalancerAttributes.postDescribeLoadBalancerAttributes
tools.description:
Describes the attributes for the specified Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer.

For more information, see the following:

  • Load balancer attributes in the Application Load Balancers Guide

  • Load balancer attributes in the Network Load Balancers Guide

  • Load balancer attributes in the Gateway Load Balancers Guide

tools.name: actionDescribeLoadBalancers.getDescribeLoadBalancers
tools.description: Describes the specified load balancers or all of your load balancers.

tools.name: actionDescribeLoadBalancers.postDescribeLoadBalancers
tools.description: Describes the specified load balancers or all of your load balancers.

tools.name: actionDescribeRules.getDescribeRules
tools.description: Describes the specified rules or the rules for the specified listener. You must specify either a listener or one or more rules.

tools.name: actionDescribeRules.postDescribeRules
tools.description: Describes the specified rules or the rules for the specified listener. You must specify either a listener or one or more rules.

tools.name: actionDescribeSslPolicies.getDescribeSslPolicies
tools.description:
Describes the specified policies or all policies used for SSL negotiation.

For more information, see Security policies in the Application Load Balancers Guide or Security policies in the Network Load Balancers Guide.

tools.name: actionDescribeSslPolicies.postDescribeSslPolicies
tools.description:
Describes the specified policies or all policies used for SSL negotiation.

For more information, see Security policies in the Application Load Balancers Guide or Security policies in the Network Load Balancers Guide.

tools.name: actionDescribeTags.getDescribeTags
tools.description: Describes the tags for the specified Elastic Load Balancing resources. You can describe the tags for one or more Application Load Balancers, Network Load Balancers, Gateway Load Balancers, target groups, listeners, or rules.

tools.name: actionDescribeTags.postDescribeTags
tools.description: Describes the tags for the specified Elastic Load Balancing resources. You can describe the tags for one or more Application Load Balancers, Network Load Balancers, Gateway Load Balancers, target groups, listeners, or rules.

tools.name: actionDescribeTargetGroupAttributes.getDescribeTargetGroupAttributes
tools.description:
Describes the attributes for the specified target group.

For more information, see the following:

  • Target group attributes in the Application Load Balancers Guide

  • Target group attributes in the Network Load Balancers Guide

  • Target group attributes in the Gateway Load Balancers Guide

tools.name: actionDescribeTargetGroupAttributes.postDescribeTargetGroupAttributes
tools.description:
Describes the attributes for the specified target group.

For more information, see the following:

  • Target group attributes in the Application Load Balancers Guide

  • Target group attributes in the Network Load Balancers Guide

  • Target group attributes in the Gateway Load Balancers Guide

tools.name: actionDescribeTargetGroups.getDescribeTargetGroups
tools.description: Describes the specified target groups or all of your target groups. By default, all target groups are described. Alternatively, you can specify one of the following to filter the results: the ARN of the load balancer, the names of one or more target groups, or the ARNs of one or more target groups.

tools.name: actionDescribeTargetGroups.postDescribeTargetGroups
tools.description: Describes the specified target groups or all of your target groups. By default, all target groups are described. Alternatively, you can specify one of the following to filter the results: the ARN of the load balancer, the names of one or more target groups, or the ARNs of one or more target groups.

tools.name: actionDescribeTargetHealth.getDescribeTargetHealth
tools.description: Describes the health of the specified targets or all of your targets.

tools.name: actionDescribeTargetHealth.postDescribeTargetHealth
tools.description: Describes the health of the specified targets or all of your targets.

tools.name: actionModifyListener.getModifyListener
tools.description:
Replaces the specified properties of the specified listener. Any properties that you do not specify remain unchanged.

Changing the protocol from HTTPS to HTTP, or from TLS to TCP, removes the security policy and default certificate properties. If you change the protocol from HTTP to HTTPS, or from TCP to TLS, you must add the security policy and default certificate properties.

To add an item to a list, remove an item from a list, or update an item in a list, you must provide the entire list. For example, to add an action, specify a list with the current actions plus the new action.

tools.name: actionModifyListener.postModifyListener
tools.description:
Replaces the specified properties of the specified listener. Any properties that you do not specify remain unchanged.

Changing the protocol from HTTPS to HTTP, or from TLS to TCP, removes the security policy and default certificate properties. If you change the protocol from HTTP to HTTPS, or from TCP to TLS, you must add the security policy and default certificate properties.

To add an item to a list, remove an item from a list, or update an item in a list, you must provide the entire list. For example, to add an action, specify a list with the current actions plus the new action.

tools.name: actionModifyLoadBalancerAttributes.getModifyLoadBalancerAttributes
tools.description:
Modifies the specified attributes of the specified Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer.

If any of the specified attributes can't be modified as requested, the call fails. Any existing attributes that you do not modify retain their current values.

tools.name: actionModifyLoadBalancerAttributes.postModifyLoadBalancerAttributes
tools.description:
Modifies the specified attributes of the specified Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer.

If any of the specified attributes can't be modified as requested, the call fails. Any existing attributes that you do not modify retain their current values.

tools.name: actionModifyRule.getModifyRule
tools.description:
Replaces the specified properties of the specified rule. Any properties that you do not specify are unchanged.

To add an item to a list, remove an item from a list, or update an item in a list, you must provide the entire list. For example, to add an action, specify a list with the current actions plus the new action.

tools.name: actionModifyRule.postModifyRule
tools.description:
Replaces the specified properties of the specified rule. Any properties that you do not specify are unchanged.

To add an item to a list, remove an item from a list, or update an item in a list, you must provide the entire list. For example, to add an action, specify a list with the current actions plus the new action.

tools.name: actionModifyTargetGroup.getModifyTargetGroup
tools.description: Modifies the health checks used when evaluating the health state of the targets in the specified target group.

tools.name: actionModifyTargetGroup.postModifyTargetGroup
tools.description: Modifies the health checks used when evaluating the health state of the targets in the specified target group.

tools.name: actionModifyTargetGroupAttributes.getModifyTargetGroupAttributes
tools.description: Modifies the specified attributes of the specified target group.

tools.name: actionModifyTargetGroupAttributes.postModifyTargetGroupAttributes
tools.description: Modifies the specified attributes of the specified target group.

tools.name: actionRegisterTargets.getRegisterTargets
tools.description:
Registers the specified targets with the specified target group.

If the target is an EC2 instance, it must be in the running state when you register it.

By default, the load balancer routes requests to registered targets using the protocol and port for the target group. Alternatively, you can override the port for a target when you register it. You can register each EC2 instance or IP address with the same target group multiple times using different ports.

With a Network Load Balancer, you cannot register instances by instance ID if they have the following instance types: C1, CC1, CC2, CG1, CG2, CR1, CS1, G1, G2, HI1, HS1, M1, M2, M3, and T1. You can register instances of these types by IP address.

tools.name: actionRegisterTargets.postRegisterTargets
tools.description:
Registers the specified targets with the specified target group.

If the target is an EC2 instance, it must be in the running state when you register it.

By default, the load balancer routes requests to registered targets using the protocol and port for the target group. Alternatively, you can override the port for a target when you register it. You can register each EC2 instance or IP address with the same target group multiple times using different ports.

With a Network Load Balancer, you cannot register instances by instance ID if they have the following instance types: C1, CC1, CC2, CG1, CG2, CR1, CS1, G1, G2, HI1, HS1, M1, M2, M3, and T1. You can register instances of these types by IP address.

tools.name: actionRemoveListenerCertificates.getRemoveListenerCertificates
tools.description: Removes the specified certificate from the certificate list for the specified HTTPS or TLS listener.

tools.name: actionRemoveListenerCertificates.postRemoveListenerCertificates
tools.description: Removes the specified certificate from the certificate list for the specified HTTPS or TLS listener.

tools.name: actionRemoveTags.getRemoveTags
tools.description: Removes the specified tags from the specified Elastic Load Balancing resources. You can remove the tags for one or more Application Load Balancers, Network Load Balancers, Gateway Load Balancers, target groups, listeners, or rules.

tools.name: actionRemoveTags.postRemoveTags
tools.description: Removes the specified tags from the specified Elastic Load Balancing resources. You can remove the tags for one or more Application Load Balancers, Network Load Balancers, Gateway Load Balancers, target groups, listeners, or rules.

tools.name: actionSetIpAddressType.getSetIpAddressType
tools.description: Sets the type of IP addresses used by the subnets of the specified load balancer.

tools.name: actionSetIpAddressType.postSetIpAddressType
tools.description: Sets the type of IP addresses used by the subnets of the specified load balancer.

tools.name: actionSetRulePriorities.getSetRulePriorities
tools.description:
Sets the priorities of the specified rules.

You can reorder the rules as long as there are no priority conflicts in the new order. Any existing rules that you do not specify retain their current priority.

tools.name: actionSetRulePriorities.postSetRulePriorities
tools.description:
Sets the priorities of the specified rules.

You can reorder the rules as long as there are no priority conflicts in the new order. Any existing rules that you do not specify retain their current priority.

tools.name: actionSetSecurityGroups.getSetSecurityGroups
tools.description:
Associates the specified security groups with the specified Application Load Balancer. The specified security groups override the previously associated security groups.

You can't specify a security group for a Network Load Balancer or Gateway Load Balancer.

tools.name: actionSetSecurityGroups.postSetSecurityGroups
tools.description:
Associates the specified security groups with the specified Application Load Balancer. The specified security groups override the previously associated security groups.

You can't specify a security group for a Network Load Balancer or Gateway Load Balancer.

tools.name: actionSetSubnets.getSetSubnets
tools.description:
Enables the Availability Zones for the specified public subnets for the specified Application Load Balancer or Network Load Balancer. The specified subnets replace the previously enabled subnets.

When you specify subnets for a Network Load Balancer, you must include all subnets that were enabled previously, with their existing configurations, plus any additional subnets.

tools.name: actionSetSubnets.postSetSubnets
tools.description:
Enables the Availability Zones for the specified public subnets for the specified Application Load Balancer or Network Load Balancer. The specified subnets replace the previously enabled subnets.

When you specify subnets for a Network Load Balancer, you must include all subnets that were enabled previously, with their existing configurations, plus any additional subnets.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
